item.title: 7/5: Face The Nation
item.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," Ed O'Keefe speaks to NCAA President Charlie Baker. Meanwhile, Reps. Adriano Espaillat and Carlos Gimenez discuss immigration and their American stories.

item.title: Trump touts American "golden age," attacks communism in July 4th speech
item.description: In a speech delayed by a two-hour evacuation of the National Mall due to severe weather, President Trump mixed politics with history.

item.title: Mallory McMorrow suspends Senate campaign in Michigan
item.description: State Sen. Mallory McMorrow announced on Sunday that she is suspending her campaign for Senate, narrowing the Democratic field ahead of the competitive August primary.

item.title: Extended interview: J.K. Simmons
item.description: In this web exclusive, the character actor who won an Academy Award for "Whiplash" talks with correspondent Tracy Smith about his roles, the epitomes of real characters.

item.title: Massive funeral for Iran's slain supreme leader enters second day
item.description: The dayslong funeral for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed in February by U.S.-Israeli strikes, has been marked by chants for revenge.

item.title: Transcript: Reps. Adriano Espaillat and Carlos Gimenez on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," July 5, 2026
item.description: The following is the full transcript of an interview with Reps. Adriano Espaillat, Democrat of New York, and Carlos Gimenez, Republican of Florida, a portion of which aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on July 5, 2026. The interview was taped on July 2, 2026.

item.title: Transcript: Former CDC chief medical officer Dr. Debra Houry on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," July 5, 2026
item.description: The following is the full transcript of an interview with former CDC chief medical officer Dr. Debra Houry, a portion of which aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on July 5, 2026. This interview was taped on July 1, 2026.

item.title: Balogun suspension lifted ahead of World Cup match against Belgium
item.description: U.S. Soccer said that it is "pleased" lead scorer Folarin Balogun will be able to compete against Belgium in Seattle, Washington.

item.title: Full transcript of "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," July 5, 2026
item.description: On this "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" broadcast, Reps. Adriano Espaillat and Carlos Giminez join Ed O'Keefe.

item.title: Yemen's Houthis kill 16 government troops; cargo ship reports attack in Red Sea
item.description: Both incidents were reported near the port city of Hodeidah, which is under control of the Iranian-backed Houthi rebel group.

item.title: NASA administrator: "We are very much in a space race right now" against China
item.description: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman said the U.S. is "very much in a space race right now" against China, as the two countries vie to land astronauts on the moon and secure a near-permanent presence.

item.title: New evacuation orders after "significant growth" on Willow Fire
item.description: Authorities overseeing the firefight against the week-old Willow Fire west of Leadville issued new evacuation and pre-evacuation orders Sunday morning after the blaze grew by nearly 1,000 acres in 24 hours.

item.title: D.C. air quality drops to "very unhealthy" levels after July 4th fireworks
item.description: A "purple" air quality alert was issued for Washington, D.C., and surrounding areas, meaning pollution reached levels considered "very unhealthy."

item.title: Charlie Kirk's alleged killer will have his preliminary hearing this week
item.description: Prosecutors will present their case this week against Tyler Robinson, the man accused of killing conservative activist Charlie Kirk.

item.title: Ex-CDC official describes "pure chaos" as RFK Jr. sought to transform agency
item.description: Former CDC chief medical officer Dr. Debra Houry warned the "scientific integrity" of federal health agencies are at risk.

item.title: Delta Air Lines flight hit by firework while landing at Midway International Airport
item.description: A Delta Air Lines flight landing in Chicago was hit by a firework as it was coming to land at Midway International Airport on Saturday.

item.title: Former CDC official describes "pure chaos" as RFK Jr. sought to transform health agency
item.description: Dr. Debra Houry, who served as chief medical officer at the CDC before leaving the agency in protest after then CDC director Susan Monarez was fired last year, told "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that there was "pure chaos" when HHS Director Robert F. Kennedy took over, and she warned that the "scientific integrity" of federal health agencies are at risk.

item.title: Face the Nation: Baker, Houry
item.description: Missed the second half of the show? NCAA President Charlie Baker and former CDC medical officer Dr. Debra Houry join.

item.title: Transcript: NASA administrator Jared Isaacman on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," July 5, 2026
item.description: The following is the transcript of an interview with NASA administrator Jared Isaacman that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on July 5, 2026.

item.title: Full Interview: Reps. Adriano Espaillat and Carlos Gimenez
item.description: Watch Ed O'Keefe's full interview with Reps. Adriano Espaillat, Democrat of New York, and Carlos Gimenez, Republican of Florida. A portion of this interview aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on July 5, 2026. Editor's note: This interview was taped on July 2, 2026.

item.title: Full interview: Former CDC chief medical officer Dr. Debra Houry
item.description: Watch Margaret Brennan's full interview with former CDC chief medical officer Dr. Debra Houry, a portion of which aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on July 5, 2025. Editor's note: This interview was taped on July 1, 2026.

item.title: NASA administrator says "we are very much in a space race right now" against China
item.description: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman told "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that the U.S. is "very much in a space race right now" against China, adding that the "Chinese are moving at incredible speeds."

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (July 5)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: U.S. military suspends search for sailor who went missing in Arabian Sea
item.description: The U.S. military said it has suspended the search for a missing sailor who was aboard a helicopter that went down in the Arabian Sea last week.

item.title: NCAA President Charlie Baker says player compensation is a "good thing" despite being "messy"
item.description: NCAA President Charlie Baker tells Ed O'Keefe on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that despite the "messy" rollout of player compensation in collegiate sports, he thinks "it's a good thing."

item.title: NCAA president says he hopes to "dramatically limit" prop betting
item.description: NCAA President Charlie Baker said he hopes to "dramatically limit" prop betting at the collegiate level and beyond.

item.title: Trump holds separate calls with Putin and Zelenskyy on ending Ukraine war
item.description: President Trump held separate calls with Russia's Vladimir Putin and Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskyy to discuss ending the war.

item.title: The battle over Stars and Stripes
item.description: The newspaper for the American military has long taken pride in its editorial independence. But under the Trump administration, restrictions have been imposed, and the Pentagon's chief spokesman has vowed to rid the paper of "woke distractions."

item.title: 7/5: Sunday Morning
item.description: Hosted by Jane Pauley. Featured: The military newspaper Star and Stripes, actor J.K. Simmons, "Little House on the Prairie" returns to screens, Founding Father Thomas Paine, a visit to George Washington's Mount Vernon, and hifi listening bars.

item.title: The Uplift: America 250
item.description: Celebrate the 4th of July and America's 250th anniversary with inspiring stories about America's rarest artifacts, most resilient small towns and loyal patriots.
